summary: sensivity ships a FiveM/GTA-V cheat with hidden Windows persistence, host fingerprinting, and obfuscated eval'd payload
details: The package is a FiveM/GTA-V game cheat ('Forte/Sensivity') wrapped in a Node.js launcher with multiple installer-hostile behaviors. launcher.js executes `eval(fs.readFileSync('server.obf.js'))` to run a 140KB obfuscator.io-protected blob (string-array + RC4 + control-flow flattening) that hides the runtime behavior from review. The decoded server collects a Windows machine fingerprint — MachineGuid (read from HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid via PowerShell), os.hostname(), and volume serial number — SHA256s them into an HWID, and POSTs `{key, hwid, nonce, app:'forte', version}` to a hardcoded author-controlled licensing endpoint. launcher.js installs a Windows Run-key persistence entry under HKCU\...\Run named 'OneDriveUpdate' (Microsoft-impersonating camouflage) that runs `wscript OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs`, which silently spawns `node.exe launcher.js` with windowsHide=true at every login. The launcher additionally respawns itself as a detached hidden supervisor with `process.title='Runtime Broker'` (another Windows-component impersonation) and stdio:'ignore'. cli.js' `update` subcommand blanket-kills all node.exe and powershell.exe processes on the host (`taskkill /F /IM node.exe`, `taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe`) before reinstalling the package, disrupting unrelated workloads. Native sens.node (6.85MB Windows PE) drives the cheat with strings like 'GTAProcess','FiveM','Teleport','LeftHand'. Installer harm: persistent hidden autostart disguised as a Microsoft component, machine-identifying data exfiltration to an author server, eval of obfuscated code that can ship arbitrary logic in future versions, and destructive process-killing on update.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
